calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-29-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-10-2022 Continued To Jul-13-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Apr-29-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Matt Loker, Esq. (805.994.0177; matt@loker.law) of Loker Law, APC for Plaintiff.
Rebecca Saelao, Esq. (415.398.3344; rss@severson.com) of Severson & Werson for Defendant, Credit Acceptance Corporation.
Alexis Jugan, Esq. (949.756.9050; ajugan@madisonlawapc.com) of Madison Law for Defendant United Auto Credit Corporation.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CREDIT ACCEPTANCE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Defendant CREDIT ACCEPTANCE CORPORATION's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied without prejudice. To establish a valid agreement to arbitrate disputes, "[t]he petitioner bears the burden of proving the existence of a valid arbitration agreement by [a] preponderance of the evidence, and a party opposing the petition bears the burden of proving by a preponderance of the evidence any fact necessary to its defense." (Engalla v. Permanente Medical Group, Inc. (1997) 15 Cal.4th 951, 972; see also Esparza v. Sand & Sea, Inc. (2016) 2 Cal.App.5th 781, 787 [same].) Here, the weight of the evidence shows that plaintiff never agreed to arbitrate any dispute with defendant. (Jackson Decl., pars. 4-22.) The motion is therefore denied without prejudice.

Parties to meet and confer to agree on a proposed order to 1) include the court's adopted ruling and 2) terms of discovery time and scope.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)
